matches.title: Iraqi Traditional Kiddush Cup – Iris Tutnauer
matches.snippet: The Iraqi Kiddush cup design is inspired by the traditional Iraqi Kiddush cups, which have a cover. Some traditional cups have a bird on the top (see photo), and others have a Magen David. The Kiddush cup with the red cloth was created for the Judaica Now: Goblets and Kiddush Cups of the Bezalel School exhibition held in Rishon L’Zion, 2016.

matches.title: Sotheby’s To Auction Earliest Known Kiddush Cup
matches.snippet: Apr 6, 2025 · The Kiddush cup, which has an estimated value of $3 million-$5 million, will be auctioned in New York on October 29. By Grace Gilson, JTA A Kiddush cup that may have been passed around the Friday night dinner table during the reign of Genghis Khan will be available for auction this fall. The rare remnant of the Silk Road, the ancient Asian trading route that was home to vibrant Jewish ...
